evidence: Developing, testing, and deploying resource-intensive AI systems may require additional investment and increase the Companies costs.
Generative AI technologies are still in their early stages of development and deployment.
AI is an emerging area of technology that has the potential to impact various aspects of the Companies business operations and customer interactions.
Generative AI technologies are still in their early stages of development and deployment.
AI algorithms that the Companies or their third-party vendors use may be flawed or may be based on datasets that are biased or insufficient.

While the Companies seek contractual protections with third-party vendors regarding the use of AI technology, the Companies may not have full awareness of, or control or visibility over, the quality, performance, security or compliance.
The rapidly evolving nature of AI technologies may cause new laws and regulations to be enacted which could dramatically affect business practices, including the costs to comply.

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No named AI systems, vendors, dollar amounts, or quantified commitments disclosed; only forward-looking cost language present.
ai_narrative_concreteness: Excerpts contain only aspirational and risk-framing language; no named initiatives, deployed use cases, or metrics disclosed.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Risk-section material is present and discusses AI with company-specific mechanisms (vendor opacity, algorithmic bias, regulatory cost, operational disruption), justifying score 4; however, no quantified risk exposure or named governance body is disclosed, precluding score 5.
